meta.title: Key — Gameplay Programmer · Unreal Engine 5 / C++
meta.description: Gameplay programmer in Tokyo with 5+ years shipping PC, console, and mobile games in Unreal Engine 5 / C++ and Unity / C#. Currently developing an unannounced AAA action RPG for PS5, Xbox Series X|S, and Steam.

hero.intro1: Building gameplay systems for PC, console, and mobile games using Unreal Engine 5, C++, Unity, and C#.
hero.intro2: 5+ years of professional experience in gameplay systems, multiplayer, UI, optimization, and cross-platform development.

unannounced-arpg.title: Unannounced AAA Action RPG
unannounced-arpg.overview: An unannounced AAA action RPG in development at Blast Edge Games for Steam, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X|S. I work across the event, cinematic, and UI layers of the game in Unreal Engine 5 and C++. Title and details are under NDA.
unannounced-arpg.confidentialNote: This title has not been announced. Artwork is a placeholder and project details are limited to what my resume already states.
contributions: Developed and polished gameplay events, cinematic sequences, and interactive gameplay systems.
Implemented UI features and fixed gameplay-related bugs.
Improved character behaviors, animations, and camera presentation.
Implemented and tuned audio, environmental effects, and interactive objects.
Addressed progression blockers, collision issues, and playtest feedback.

challenges.challenge: Cinematic sequences must hand control back and forth with interactive gameplay without ever stranding the player.
challenges.solution: Implemented and hardened event flows that transition between cinematic presentation and player control, fixing state edge cases surfaced by playtests.

challenges.challenge: Playtests surfaced progression blockers and collision issues across a large, dense game world.
challenges.solution: Reproduced reports systematically, fixed root causes in gameplay and collision code, and verified fixes against playtest feedback loops.

hyke.title: HYKE: Northern Light(s)
hyke.overview: As a Gameplay Programmer at Blast Edge Games, I contributed to HYKE: Northern Light(s) by implementing gameplay features, character functionality, debugging gameplay issues, and optimizing memory and Game Thread performance across multiple platforms.
contributions: Optimized Game Thread performance on Nintendo Switch.

challenges.challenge: The iOS build was crashing due to insufficient memory.
challenges.solution: Investigated and optimized memory usage to resolve the crashes. I wrote up the process as a technical blog post on UE5 memory optimization.

links.label: [UE5] Useful Tools, Commands, and Methods for Memory Optimization

challenges.challenge: Game Thread load on Nintendo Switch was too high, causing unstable performance.
challenges.solution: Profiled the game and reduced Game Thread cost on Switch. I also compiled a set of notes while learning UE5 optimization more broadly.

apeiron.title: Apeiron
apeiron.overview: As Lead Programmer of a five-person gameplay team, I designed and implemented core gameplay systems including combat mechanics, card systems, enemy AI, and multiplayer synchronization for this action RTS card battler.
contributions: Developed gameplay systems, combat mechanics, card systems, and enemy AI in C#.
Mentored junior programmers and participated in technical interviews.
Assisted with project planning and scheduling.

challenges.challenge: Card effects triggering inside real-time RTS combat had to stay consistent across networked clients.
challenges.solution: Designed the gameplay state flow around a Socket.IO synchronization layer and hardened it against desyncs surfaced in playtesting.

challenges.challenge: A deep, expanding card system had to remain maintainable for a 5-person team.
challenges.solution: Owned the system design: data-driven card and skill definitions so new content shipped without touching core combat code.

every-hero.title: Every Hero
every-hero.overview: As a Gameplay Programmer at Feeling Game Company, I developed the Equipment System, Character Skill System, and Main Menu UI for Every Hero, a mobile rogue-lite action RPG built with Unity. The game surpassed one million players and reached #2 in the App Store Action Games category.
contributions: Developed gameplay systems, combat mechanics, equipment systems, and UI.
Optimized game performance across a wide range of mobile devices.
Performed QA, Japanese localization, and advertisement integration.

challenges.challenge: A deep equipment and skill system had to stay maintainable with only five developers.
challenges.solution: Built data-driven inventory, equipment, and skill systems in C# so designers could extend content without engineering time.

challenges.challenge: Action gameplay with many entities had to hold frame rate on low-spec phones.
challenges.solution: Profiled and optimized gameplay and UI code paths to keep performance stable across devices.

bullets: Developing gameplay events, cinematics, UI, and interactive systems for an unannounced AAA action RPG (Steam / PS5 / Xbox Series X|S) in Unreal Engine 5 and C++.
Improved character behaviors, animations, and camera presentation; fixed progression blockers and playtest findings.
Optimized HYKE: Northern Light(s) — memory usage on iOS and Game Thread performance on Nintendo Switch.
Working with Perforce in a large multi-team codebase.
GUARDS! (Steam): gameplay QA, debugging, and Japanese localization in a 4-person multiplayer indie team (UE, C++).
Danmaku shooting game: developed gameplay systems, combat, enemy AI, and UI in Unity / C#.
Doki Doki House (iOS / Steam): built a pipeline tool converting Excel scripts to Ren'Py code for a title with 50,000+ iOS players.
Apeiron (Steam / Epic): developed gameplay systems, combat mechanics, card systems, and enemy AI in C#.
Mentored junior programmers, joined technical interviews, and assisted project planning.
Every Hero (iOS / Android): developed gameplay, combat, equipment systems, and UI in C#.
Optimized performance; performed QA, Japanese localization, and ad integration.
Contributed to a title with 1M+ players and an App Store Action Games #2 ranking.

polyu.detail: Specialized in AI and machine learning; coursework in game design and game development.

chuhai.detail: Algorithms, data structures, OOP, databases, and software engineering.

danmaku.desc: Shoot-'em-up prototype turning real Bilibili video comments (danmaku) into oncoming enemies. Built in a 3-person team.

hook-racer.desc: Grappling-hook racing action. 7-week solo project — all gameplay, pixel art, and music. Award winner at the 24th UE5ぷちコン.

shadow-bubble.title: Shadow Bubble: Hide and Seek Chaos
shadow-bubble.desc: Two-player asymmetric stealth in 7 days. Audience Choice & Best XR Game, GGJ Hong Kong 2025.

teacup.title: Nesmy Land: Teacup
teacup.desc: Action-simulation built in 5 days in a 3-person team — gameplay programming and level design.

path-of-osu.title: The Path of Osu
path-of-osu.desc: Third-person action-adventure prototype with combat and puzzles, solo-developed over 7 weeks.

blite.title: Blite
blite.desc: Puzzle platformer built in 2 days. Best Original Game, GGJ Hong Kong 2022.

independent.title: Independent Projects
independent.description: Projects developed independently to explore gameplay ideas, tools, and technical experimentation beyond my professional work.

guards.title: GUARDS!
guards.desc: QA testing and Japanese localization for a released Unreal Engine title on Steam.

doki-doki-house.title: Doki Doki House
doki-doki-house.desc: Developed Python tools and implemented narrative systems for a Ren'Py visual novel game.

hero-race.title: Hero Race (Warcraft III)
hero-race.desc: An original Warcraft III custom map that I have continuously designed, balanced, and programmed since 2011, serving as my foundation in gameplay programming, scripting, and multiplayer game design.

gameJournal.title: Game Journal
gameJournal.lead: For over 10 years I've kept a personal archive of nearly 1,000 games — not formal reviews, but notes from both a gameplay programmer's and a player's perspective. For most entries I record genre, developer, publisher, engine (when known), platform, completion status, and a personal rating, along with what I liked about the design, its weak points, and general development notes.

blogTeaser.title: Technical blog on Unreal Engine & game development
blogTeaser.lead: I write in-depth articles on Unreal Engine 5, C++, and gameplay programming — the practical systems and techniques behind shipping games.

hobbies.title: Off hours
hobbies.text: Making and playing games, electric guitar, figure skating, anime, museums, language learning.

resume.description: Full work history, project details, and certifications in PDF format.

contact.title: Let's talk
contact.lead: Open to gameplay programming opportunities and interesting conversations about game development. The fastest way to reach me is email.

meta.title: Hero Race — 15 Years of Independent Gameplay Design
meta.description: A case study on Hero Race, a Warcraft III multiplayer racing game I have designed, programmed, and balanced continuously since 2011.

heroRace.title: Hero Race (Warcraft III)
lede: A multiplayer racing game that I have continuously designed, programmed, balanced, and maintained for over 15 years.

Unlike a one-off hobby project, Hero Race has evolved through years of live player feedback, gameplay iteration, and technical improvement — becoming my personal sandbox for gameplay programming and game design.

overview.text: Hero Race started in 2011 and remains the longest-running project in my personal body of work. I've continuously designed, programmed, and balanced it ever since, using it as my sandbox for gameplay programming and game design.

principles.title: Every Match Feels Different
principles.desc: Random items and dynamic mechanics keep every race unique.

principles.title: Player Interaction
principles.desc: Abilities create constant interaction rather than isolated racing.

principles.title: High Skill Ceiling
principles.desc: Movement, timing, and positioning reward mastery.

principles.title: High Risk, High Reward
principles.desc: Powerful abilities require commitment and create exciting moments.

principles.title: Continuous Comebacks
principles.desc: Map mechanics and items prevent races from being decided too early.

groups.title: Gameplay Systems
groups.desc: Hero abilities, race mechanics, items, and multiplayer gameplay.

groups.title: Bot Players
groups.desc: State machine–based bots for solo play, practice, and filling empty matches.

groups.title: Programming
groups.desc: JASS scripting, the trigger system, and gameplay architecture.

groups.title: Game Design
groups.desc: 15 years of balancing and iterative gameplay improvements.

community.text: I actively manage the player community, collect feedback, observe player behaviour, and continuously refine the game. Every patch is evaluated by asking one question.
community.question: Does this change still preserve the gameplay experience I originally wanted?

sourceCode.text: The GitHub repository contains the JASS-scripted state machine bot AI. The rest of the game's systems live inside the compiled .w3x map file, which requires the Warcraft III World Editor to open and can't be browsed on GitHub.
